questions.question: In ΔABC and ΔPQR, AB = PQ and BC = QR. What additional information is needed for SAS congruence?
questions.answer: The included angles must be equal, i.e., ∠B = ∠Q.

questions.question: If ΔDEF ≅ ΔBCA, write the part of ΔBCA that corresponds to side EF.
questions.answer: Side CA (or AC).

questions.question: Two triangles have equal areas. Are they necessarily congruent? Explain.
questions.answer: No. They can have different shapes (e.g., different base and height) but still have the same area.

questions.question: In a right-angled ΔABC, if the hypotenuse and one side are equal to the hypotenuse and one side of ΔXYZ, which criterion proves congruence?
questions.answer: RHS (Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side) criterion.

questions.question: If ΔPQR ≅ ΔEDF, is it true to say that PR = EF?
questions.answer: Yes, because they are corresponding parts of congruent triangles (CPCT).

questions.question: Explain why the AAA (Angle-Angle-Angle) condition is not sufficient for congruence.
questions.answer: AAA ensures the same shape (similarity), but the side lengths could be different, meaning they are not the same size.

questions.question: In ΔLMN and ΔSTU, ∠M = ∠T = 90°, LN = SU, and MN = TU. Name the congruence rule.
questions.answer: RHS Congruence rule.

questions.question: If ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR under the correspondence ABC ↔ RQP, write the side of ΔPQR that corresponds to BC.
questions.answer: Side QP.

questions.question: Can two triangles be congruent if they have two sides and a non-included angle equal?
questions.answer: No, the angle must be the included angle (SAS). SSA is not a congruence criterion.

questions.question: Given AB = DE, BC = EF, and AC = DF. Write the congruence relation in symbolic form.
questions.answer: ΔABC ≅ ΔDEF.

questions.question: In ΔABC, if ∠A = 40°, ∠B = 60° and in ΔXYZ, ∠X = 40°, ∠Y = 60°, and AB = XY, which rule applies?
questions.answer: ASA (Angle-Side-Angle) Congruence rule.

questions.question: If ΔABC ≅ ΔFED, is it correct to say ∠B = ∠E?
questions.answer: Yes, because B and E are the corresponding vertices in the given notation.

questions.question: A square has a side of 5 cm and another square has a perimeter of 20 cm. Are they congruent?
questions.answer: Yes, because the second square also has a side of 20/4 = 5 cm. Squares with equal sides are congruent.

questions.question: Why is the SSS criterion called 'Side-Side-Side'?
questions.answer: Because it requires all three corresponding sides of two triangles to be equal in length.

questions.question: If ΔPQR ≅ ΔXYZ, and PQ = 5cm, QR = 6cm, ∠Q = 50°, find XY and ∠Y.
questions.answer: XY = 5cm and ∠Y = 50° (by CPCT).

questions.question: Two line segments AB and CD are congruent. If AB = (2x + 1) cm and CD = 7 cm, find x.
In ΔABC and ΔDEF, ∠A = ∠D, ∠B = ∠E and BC = EF. Which rule can be used to prove they are congruent?
questions.answer: AAS (or ASA since the third angle must also be equal).

questions.question: If we superimpose one congruent figure over another, what happens?
questions.answer: They cover each other exactly because they have identical dimensions.

questions.question: Can a triangle be congruent to its own mirror image?
questions.answer: Yes, any triangle is congruent to its reflected image as size and shape remain unchanged.

questions.question: In an isosceles ΔABC where AB = AC, if AD is the bisector of ∠A, why is ΔABD ≅ ΔACD?
questions.answer: By SAS rule (AB = AC, ∠BAD = ∠CAD, and AD is common).

questions.question: If ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR, does it mean the perimeter of ΔABC equals the perimeter of ΔPQR?
questions.answer: Yes, because all corresponding sides are equal, their sums (perimeters) must also be equal.

questions.question: In ΔPQR, if PQ = PR and PL is the altitude to QR, which rule proves ΔPLQ ≅ ΔPLR?
questions.answer: RHS Rule (∠PLQ = ∠PLR = 90°, Hypotenuse PQ = PR, and PL is common).

questions.question: If two rectangles have equal perimeters, are they always congruent?
questions.answer: No, for example, a 4x4 rectangle and a 6x2 rectangle both have perimeter 16 but different dimensions.

questions.question: What is the condition for two angles to be congruent?
questions.answer: They must have the same degree measure.

questions.question: If ΔABC ≅ ΔXYZ, and ΔXYZ ≅ ΔPQR, is ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR?
questions.answer: Yes, congruence follows the transitive property.
